name: Avicenna
full_name: Abu Ali al-Husayn ibn Abd Allah ibn Sina

key_works.title: The Canon of Medicine (Al-Qanun fi al-Tibb)

summary: The greatest philosopher-scientist of the medieval world. Avicenna extended Aristotle's metaphysics into a complete system of Islamic philosophy, and simultaneously wrote the Canon of Medicine — a medical encyclopaedia used in European universities for six centuries. His Floating Man thought experiment anticipates Descartes by 600 years.
long_summary: Born in Bukhara (modern Uzbekistan) and working across Persia, Avicenna was a polymath of staggering range. His philosophical masterwork, The Book of Healing, covers logic, natural sciences, mathematics, and metaphysics — the largest encyclopaedia of knowledge produced by a single mind in the medieval world. His metaphysics distinguished between essence and existence (a thing can be conceived without necessarily existing), a distinction that would profoundly influence both Islamic and Christian scholastic philosophy. The Floating Man argument asks you to imagine a person suspended in empty space, deprived of all sensory input — and concludes that they would still be aware of their own existence, pointing to the soul as irreducible. His Canon of Medicine remained a standard medical text in Europe until the 17th century.
